summary: Chapter 1 drafted successfully. All three scenes executed per outline (seawall patrol with Oathbrand flare and Pallor tear, garrison wall repair with Lirien surveillance, Tavik confrontation). 4247 words. Kaelith's voice fingerprint maintained throughout: military-precise, short declarative sentences, structural metaphors, emotions as physical sensations. Foreshadowing seeds planted (FS-001 through FS-004). Mystery boxes MB-001 and MB-007 opened. Symbolic motifs MOT-001 (Pallor) and MOT-002 (Oathbrand) introduced. Emotional arc progressed from numb endurance to hyperalert threat assessment. All craft tools applied per craft profile. Tracker artifacts updated: continuity tracker, foreshadowing ledger, mystery box inventory, symbolic motif registry, information asymmetry map, emotional throughline.

INV-005: Show don't tell (emotions as physical sensations)

notes: Chapter 1 establishes story foundation successfully. Kaelith's military voice is distinct and consistent. Pallor tear and Oathbrand anomaly create immediate mystery. Lirien's introduction through surveillance creates predator/prey tension without dialogue. Tavik's transparent lie establishes institutional betrayal theme. Emotional restraint maintained throughout — no melodrama, no exposition dumps. Reader positioned to care about Kaelith's containment and threat. All required foreshadowing planted. Chapter ends on defensive calculation and resource assessment, priming for escalation in chapter 2.
